title: The Mountains in Labour
lines: Once there was a great commotion in the Mountains.
The very earth was shaken, and the rocks rolled down the sides in great heaps.
The neighboring people came out in great fright, expecting that the Mountains would produce some dreadful monster.
At last, after all this fuss, out came a Mouse.
The people laughed at the great fuss over such a tiny creature.
moral: Much fuss often comes to nothing.
